accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.subtitle: Mood UI components ship with ARIA semantics, keyboard navigation and visible focus management out of the box. Here is how we approach accessibility and what to expect.
accessibility.disclaimerBody: Mood UI is not officially audited or certified against WCAG 2.2 AA. Conformance is assessed on a best-effort basis. Accessibility in your application also depends on how you compose components — providing labels, preserving focus, and maintaining adequate contrast in your own color overrides.
accessibility.targetDesc: We design every component to meet WCAG 2.2 Level AA where applicable. This covers the four core POUR principles: Perceivable, Operable, Understandable and Robust.

contrast.title: Color contrast
contrast.text: Default color tokens meet the 4.5:1 ratio for normal text and 3:1 for large text and UI components. Custom palettes via paletteFromHex() follow the same rules.

keyboard.title: Keyboard navigation
keyboard.text: Every interactive component works without a mouse. Tab, Space, Enter, Escape and arrow keys follow established WAI-ARIA patterns.

focus.title: Focus management
focus.text: Focus is always visible. Modal, Drawer and ConfirmDialog implement a focus trap — focus stays inside the overlay and returns to the trigger on close.

aria.title: ARIA semantics
aria.text: Native HTML is preferred. ARIA roles, states and properties (dialog, combobox, aria-live, aria-expanded…) are added only when necessary.

motion.title: Reduced motion
motion.text: All transitions respect prefers-reduced-motion. When the user opts out, animations are replaced with instant state changes.

labels.title: Accessible labels
labels.text: Icon-only buttons and close buttons expose aria-label. Use the label or aria-label prop to give screen readers meaningful text.

accessibility.wcagDesc: WCAG 2.2 is the W3C standard for web accessibility. Level AA is the widely accepted baseline for enterprise and public-sector software.

perceivable.label: Perceivable
perceivable.text: Information and UI must be presentable to users in ways they can perceive — text alternatives, sufficient contrast, adaptable content.

operable.label: Operable
operable.text: UI components and navigation must be fully operable — keyboard accessible, no seizure triggers, enough time to interact, visible focus.

understandable.label: Understandable
understandable.text: Content and operation must be understandable — readable text, predictable behavior, helpful error messages.

robust.label: Robust
robust.text: Content must be interpreted by current and future assistive technologies — valid semantics, name/role/value for all UI components.

accessibility.nextDesc: Each component page includes an accessibility notes section with keyboard shortcuts, ARIA roles and focus behavior.
accessibility.nextComponentsDesc: Every component is documented with its a11y behavior.
accessibility.kbDesc: Complete keyboard shortcut reference organized by component category.

kbActions.closeOverlay: Close overlay, restore focus to trigger

accessibility.ariaDesc: Role and attribute patterns used by Mood UI components to communicate semantics to assistive technologies.

ariaPatterns.dialog: Focus-trapped overlay dialog. Background is inert while open.
ariaPatterns.combobox: Dropdown selector. Communicates expanded/collapsed state to screen readers.
ariaPatterns.radiogroup: Grouped radio inputs with arrow-key navigation between them.
ariaPatterns.switch: Binary toggle control that announces on/off state.
ariaPatterns.slider: Numeric range control that announces current, minimum and maximum values.
ariaPatterns.tablist: Tab strip with keyboard navigation and associated content panels.
ariaPatterns.live: Polite live region for dynamic announcements to screen readers.
ariaPatterns.progressbar: Progress indicator with numeric value, minimum and maximum.
ariaPatterns.tooltip: Contextual description linked to the triggering element via aria-describedby.

accessibility.testDesc: Recommended tools and a basic checklist to verify accessibility in your application.

tools.axe: Open source JS engine. Integrate in Jest/Vitest to catch issues in CI/CD pipelines.
tools.axeExt: Chrome & Firefox extension. Run on-demand audits directly in the browser DevTools.
tools.wave: Visual overlay tool. Highlights errors, alerts and structure issues on the page in real time.
tools.lighthouse: Built into Chrome DevTools. Full accessibility audit alongside performance and SEO.
tools.voiceover: Native macOS/iOS screen reader. No install needed — press Cmd+F5 to start.
tools.nvda: Free open source Windows screen reader. Most widely used with Chrome and Firefox.

checklist.item1: All interactive elements are reachable and operable by keyboard alone.
checklist.item2: Focus indicator is always visible — never hidden with outline: none.
checklist.item3: No unintentional keyboard traps outside of modal overlays.
checklist.item4: Text color contrast meets 4.5:1 (normal text) or 3:1 (large text and UI components).
checklist.item5: Non-decorative images have a meaningful alt attribute.
checklist.item6: Dynamic state changes (open, selected, error) are announced to screen readers.
checklist.item7: Page remains fully usable at 200% browser zoom without horizontal scrolling.
checklist.item8: Error messages are programmatically linked to their inputs via aria-describedby.

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.description: Every release of mood-ui listed newest first. SemVer-tracked, with highlights of new components, bug fixes and breaking changes.
changelog.intro: Every release listed newest first. We follow SemVer.

v0.7.1.title: Color mode hotfix
items: useColorMode now reacts when the exposed mode ref is changed directly.
Direct assignments like mode.value = 'dark' now persist to localStorage and update the html .dark class.
System mode continues to react to OS color-scheme changes through the resolved mode watcher.
Removed the automatic GitHub Pages showroom deployment workflow.

v0.7.0.title: ColorModeSwitch + mobile UX + component fixes
items: New public component: ColorModeSwitch with full docs page.
Tooltip fully refactored — better positioning, scoped theme, arrow & delay support.
Avatar icon fallback now respects the color prop.
DateRangeField auto-collapses to 1-month view on mobile.
Combobox: added touchstart support for mobile devices.
PopoverPanel: switched to overflow-y-auto so tall content scrolls correctly.
ToastContainer: CSS variable style now propagates to all zones.
CalendarMonthView: improved draggable event rendering.
Theme Studio customizer redesigned to match ShowroomSettings style.
Mobile header: section pill moved to right cluster next to color mode switch.
Mobile drawer now includes search and language switcher.
Fixed: mobile section sheet no longer shows Documentation as active when on Home.
i18n: extracted all remaining hardcoded strings from showroom pages (ES/EN).

v0.6.0.title: Docs overhaul + full i18n
items: ComponentDoc + ComponentPreview system with isolated ModoProvider.
Migrated ~50 component pages to ComponentDoc system.
Full ES/EN translations for all component pages.
TOC scroll-spy for navigation.
Reset button on ComponentPreview toolbar.
Unified design system across all doc pages.

v0.5.2.title: Showroom polish + a11y
items: New surface-preset picker inside the settings popover.
Search palette now uses the native SearchInput component.
View transitions between routes (Chromium / Safari TP).
Lighter welcome headline with scroll-reveal animations.
"Star on GitHub" button in the header, translated ES/EN.

v0.5.0.title: Templates + theme studio
items: Templates: Dashboard, Auth, Pricing and Settings.
Interactive Theme Studio with live preview.
New Scheduler component with drag & drop.
Support for 6 dark surface presets.

v0.4.0.title: Calendar suite + Table
items: Calendar, MonthView, WeekView, DayView, AgendaView.
Table: virtualization, multi-sort, server-side, tree rows.
DateRangePicker and DateTimePicker.

v0.3.0.title: Dark surfaces + i18n
items: Dark surface presets (navy, zinc, charcoal, midnight, forest).
Built-in i18n with ES/EN dicts.
useToast / useConfirm composables.

v0.1.0.title: First public release
items: 40+ base components.
ModoProvider with reactive theming.
Tailwind v4 + design tokens.

next.themingDesc: Tokens, palettes, surfaces and dark mode.

i18n.title: i18n
i18n.subtitle: mood-ui includes full internationalization support with Spanish and English built-in. Change language globally, override per component or create full locales for other languages.
i18n.presetDesc: The simplest way: import {code} and pass the preset you want to the provider.
i18n.dynamicDesc: {code} is a reactive singleton. Any call to {set} updates the language globally across the entire app.
i18n.dynamicTip: Tip: The singleton is module-level, so {code} in any component returns the same state. You don't need Pinia or stores.
i18n.partialDesc: {code} lets you override only the strings you need. The rest are inherited from the default (English).
i18n.customDesc: Use {code} to combine your locale with the default. Ensures all strings have an English fallback.
i18n.namespacesDesc: The {code} type is organized into namespaces, one per component or category:
i18n.themingCardDesc: Tokens, palettes, surfaces and dark mode presets.

installation.title: Installation
installation.subtitle: Mood-UI is installed as a standard npm package. Compatible with Vue 3.5+ and Tailwind CSS v4.
installation.step2Desc: Make sure you have Vue 3.5+ and Tailwind CSS v4 installed:
installation.step3Desc: Import {code} after Tailwind. This registers the design tokens and all utilities used by the components.
installation.step3Tip: Tip: mood-ui uses {themeInline} to map its CSS vars to Tailwind tokens. You don't need to configure anything in {tailwindConfig}.
installation.step4Desc: {code} propagates theming, language and tokens to all descendants. Only one is needed per application (though you can nest them for local themes).
installation.step5Desc: Import any component from the package root. Everything is tree-shakeable, so only what you actually use is included in your bundle.
installation.themeCardDesc: Color, radius, surfaces, dark mode presets…

introduction.title: Mood-UI
introduction.intro: A Vue 3 component library with {n}+ accessible pieces, reactive theming and dark mode with customizable surfaces. Built to ship fast without fighting your design system.

theming.title: Reactive theming
theming.text: Every token is a CSS var. Change color, radius, halo or density and every component reacts instantly — no remount.

i18n.title: Built-in i18n
i18n.text: Spanish and English dictionaries by default, with an API to extend or override any string.

a11y.title: Accessible by default
a11y.text: ARIA roles, visible focus rings, keyboard nav and verified contrast ratios — no extra work.

treeShake.title: Tree-shakeable
treeShake.text: Import only what you use. Minimal initial bundle, modern ESM, full types.

tailwind.title: Tailwind v4 native
tailwind.text: \@theme inline + design tokens. Override from your own config without hacks.

ts.title: TypeScript first
ts.text: Every prop, slot and event typed. IntelliSense that actually helps.

introduction.orgIntro: The docs are split into four big areas. Start with installation, take a tour through theming, then jump into the component catalog.

theming.desc: Tokens, palettes, surfaces and dark mode.

i18n.desc: Switch languages at runtime, extend dicts.

theming.title: Theming
theming.subtitle: mood-ui uses a configuration cascade based on CSS custom properties + Tailwind v4 tokens. Customize via the provider, surfaces, generated palettes or by overriding CSS vars directly.
theming.providerPropsDesc: {code} accepts props that propagate to all descendants. Any component can locally override with its own prop.
theming.customPaletteTitle: Custom palette from a HEX color
theming.customPaletteDesc: {code} generates a full 50→900 scale from a single color. Useful for brands with a single primary color.
theming.surfacesDesc: Override all 14 semantic tokens directly. Ideal for custom themes.
theming.cssOverrideDesc: If you prefer to work 100% in CSS, override the custom properties in your global stylesheet. Works with {root}, {dark} or any selector.
theming.cssOverrideTip: Recommendation: If you have reactive logic (e.g. color change from a picker), use {palettes} or {surfaces} in the Provider. For static themes, CSS override is the simplest approach.
theming.componentsCardDesc: 70+ components ready to use with reactive theming.
theming.i18nCardDesc: ES and EN included. Extend for any language.
